questions.q: Who founded the Forward Bloc?
questions.explanation: The Forward Bloc was founded by Subhas Chandra Bose after he left the Congress presidency.

questions.q: In which year was the Forward Bloc founded?
questions.explanation: Subhas Chandra Bose founded the Forward Bloc in 1939.

questions.q: The Forward Bloc was formed after Bose resigned from which position?
questions.explanation: Bose formed the Forward Bloc in 1939 after resigning from the Congress presidency.

questions.q: The Forward Bloc was originally formed as a faction within which larger organization?
questions.explanation: The Forward Bloc began as a left-wing bloc within the Indian National Congress before becoming a separate party.

questions.q: What was the primary political aim of the Forward Bloc?
questions.explanation: The Forward Bloc pressed for uncompromising opposition to British rule and sought to unite the more radical elements of the movement.

questions.q: The Forward Bloc sought to unite which section of the freedom movement?
questions.explanation: The Forward Bloc aimed to bring together the more radical and left-wing elements of the freedom movement.

questions.q: By the popular title 'Netaji', the founder of the Forward Bloc is best known. Who was he?
questions.explanation: Subhas Chandra Bose, who founded the Forward Bloc, is popularly remembered as Netaji.

questions.q: In which Indian province did Bose first announce and base the Forward Bloc?
questions.explanation: Bose, a Bengali leader, launched the Forward Bloc from Bengal, where it drew its strongest early support.

questions.q: The Forward Bloc emerged during the late 1930s, a period when Bose disagreed with the Congress leadership over the pace of the struggle against which power?
questions.explanation: The Forward Bloc was created amid Bose's differences with the Congress leadership over the struggle against British rule.

questions.q: The ideology of the Forward Bloc combined nationalism with which broad economic outlook?
questions.explanation: The Forward Bloc combined assertive nationalism with a broadly socialist economic outlook.

questions.q: At which 1939 Congress session did Bose defeat the candidate backed by Gandhi, an event that preceded the founding of the Forward Bloc?
questions.explanation: At the Tripuri session of 1939 Bose won re-election against Pattabhi Sitaramayya, whom Gandhi supported, and soon after founded the Forward Bloc.

questions.q: Whom did Bose defeat in the 1939 Congress presidential election before tensions led to the Forward Bloc?
questions.explanation: Bose defeated Pattabhi Sitaramayya, the candidate favoured by Gandhi, in the 1939 Congress presidential contest.

questions.q: What was the name of the weekly newspaper started by Bose as a mouthpiece for the Forward Bloc?
questions.explanation: Bose launched a weekly publication titled Forward Bloc to spread the party's ideas.

questions.q: In which month of 1939 did Bose formally announce the creation of the Forward Bloc?
questions.explanation: Bose announced the formation of the Forward Bloc in early May 1939.

questions.q: The first all-India conference of the Forward Bloc in 1940 was held in which city?
questions.explanation: The Forward Bloc held its first all-India conference at Nagpur in 1940, where it took shape as a distinct party.

questions.q: Bose's founding of the Forward Bloc reflected his preference for which kind of approach over Gandhi's strategy?
options: Cooperation with the British war effort
questions.explanation: Bose favoured a more assertive and militant approach to independence than the gradualist line he associated with the Congress mainstream.

questions.q: After leaving for Europe and Asia in the early 1940s, Bose's later organisation of an army abroad overshadowed the Forward Bloc. What was that army called?
questions.explanation: Bose went on to lead the Indian National Army (Azad Hind Fauj) abroad, an effort that eclipsed his earlier Forward Bloc work in India.

questions.q: The Forward Bloc described itself as standing on the left within Indian politics. Which of these best captures that positioning?
options: A loyalist body supporting the Raj
questions.explanation: The Forward Bloc positioned itself as a leftist and radical nationalist party committed to complete independence.

questions.q: During the Second World War, the Forward Bloc's stance toward the British was to oppose Indian participation in the war. This was consistent with the party's call for what?
options: Loyal support of the war effort
questions.explanation: The Forward Bloc consistently demanded uncompromising opposition to British rule and resisted cooperation with the wartime government.

questions.q: Which slogan associated with Bose captured the militant spirit that the Forward Bloc championed?
options: Give me blood, and I shall give you freedom
questions.explanation: Bose's famous exhortation 'Give me blood, and I shall give you freedom' reflected the militant spirit underlying his Forward Bloc and later activities.

questions.q: Before founding the Forward Bloc, Bose had served two terms as Congress president. In which years was he elected?
questions.explanation: Bose was elected Congress president in 1938 (Haripura) and again in 1939 (Tripuri) before resigning and founding the Forward Bloc.

questions.q: The Forward Bloc adopted as one of its goals the achievement of which form of freedom?
questions.explanation: Like Bose himself, the Forward Bloc set complete independence as its objective rather than any limited autonomy.

questions.q: Bose initially conceived the Forward Bloc as a rallying platform for left elements inside the Congress. What was a key reason it could not remain within the Congress fold?
options: It was banned by the British immediately
Congress disciplinary action against Bose and his supporters
It merged with the Muslim League
questions.explanation: Disciplinary measures taken by the Congress Working Committee against Bose and his followers pushed the Forward Bloc to develop into a separate party.

questions.q: Which Congress-imposed restriction in 1939 followed the Tripuri controversy and contributed to Bose's break and the rise of the Forward Bloc?
options: A fine on the Bengal Congress
A ban on Bose holding any elective Congress office for three years
Dissolution of the Congress Working Committee
questions.explanation: Bose was barred from holding an elective Congress office for three years, a penalty that deepened the rift and spurred the Forward Bloc.

questions.q: The Forward Bloc's early programme called for collective action and discipline modelled partly on which contemporary international ideological currents?
questions.explanation: The Forward Bloc drew on anti-imperialist and socialist currents of the era, reflecting Bose's leftist and radical leanings.

questions.q: After Bose left India in 1941, who were among the leaders that helped sustain the Forward Bloc in his absence?
options: Members of his Bengal-based associates and family circle
questions.explanation: In Bose's absence the Forward Bloc was carried on largely by his Bengal-based associates, including members of his close circle.

questions.q: The Forward Bloc's founding in 1939 came at a time when which world event was about to reshape Indian politics?
options: The outbreak of the Second World War
questions.explanation: The Forward Bloc was founded in mid-1939, just months before the Second World War broke out in September 1939 and transformed Indian politics.

questions.q: Which feature distinguished the Forward Bloc's stance from that of the Congress majority during the war years?
options: Willingness to compromise with the Raj for reforms
Insistence on immediate, militant struggle without conditions
questions.explanation: Unlike the more conditional Congress approach, the Forward Bloc pressed for immediate and militant struggle without compromise with the colonial government.

questions.q: The Forward Bloc survived as a political party in independent India, retaining its strongest presence in which state?
questions.explanation: The All India Forward Bloc continued after independence and retained its strongest base in West Bengal, Bose's home region.

questions.q: Which colour scheme and symbol did the Forward Bloc associate with itself, echoing leftist and Bose's own iconography?
options: A leaping tiger on a red background
questions.explanation: The Forward Bloc adopted a leaping tiger emblem on a red field, reflecting both its leftist colour and Bose's symbolism.
